# _site/

Hugo "plumbing" that isn't tied to any single website section. Everything that
would otherwise sit at the repo root in a vanilla Hugo project (the shared
`baseof.html`, `_default/` layouts, `_partials/`, shortcodes, archetypes, i18n,
global CSS, fonts, the Netlify `_redirects` file, etc.) lives here.

```
_site/
├── archetypes/              `hugo new` defaults (mounted at archetypes/)
├── i18n/                    translation files (mounted at i18n/)
├── static/                  files served verbatim at the site root
│   ├── _redirects           Netlify-style redirects (generated by build_redirects.py)
│   ├── files/               PDFs and other large binaries
│   └── images/              site-wide images
└── data/                    cross-section data (currently empty; section data
                             lives under each section's data/ subfolder)
```

## What's NOT here, and why

Two folders intentionally stay at the **project root** even though they're
"shared plumbing":

- **`assets/`** — the HugoBlox tailwind theme's `_partials/site_head.html`
  calls `fileExists "assets/css/custom.css"`, which checks the literal
  filesystem path and does **not** follow Hugo's mount remapping. Moving
  the folder under `_site/` would silently drop our custom CSS.
- **`layouts/`** (the shared bits: `baseof.html`, `alias.html`, `_default/`,
  `_partials/`, `shortcodes/`, `page/`) — the HugoBlox `get_hook` partial
  calls `os.ReadDir "layouts/_partials/hooks/body-end/"` to discover hook
  scripts, and `os.ReadDir` is also not mount-aware. Section-specific
  layouts (`layouts/publication/`, etc.) DO move into their section folders
  because Hugo's normal layout-lookup chain *is* mount-aware.

If you need to add a new shared partial / shortcode / default layout, put
it under `layouts/` at the repo root. If you need to add a new
section-specific layout, put it under `<section>/layouts/`.

## How Hugo sees this

- `layouts`           -> `layouts`     (shared theme bits, stays at root)
- `_site/archetypes`  -> `archetypes`
- `_site/i18n`        -> `i18n`
- `_site/static`      -> `static`
- `_site/data`        -> `data`
- `assets`            -> `assets`      (stays at root; see note above)
